title: Assumption-Diagnostic Ensemble Causal Discovery: Reconciling Algorithm Disagreements via Data-Driven Assumption Profiling
description: We propose ADECD, a framework that runs a portfolio of diverse causal discovery algorithms (PC, FCI, GES, NOTEARS, LiNGAM, CAM), computes per-edge statistical diagnostics of key assumption satisfaction (linearity, Gaussianity, faithfulness, causal sufficiency), and reconciles conflicting outputs through assumption-weighted combination with bootstrap-adaptive calibration to produce a unified causal graph with calibrated confidence scores and interpretable per-edge diagnostics.
motivation: Different causal discovery algorithms make different assumptions and produce conflicting graphs on the same data. Practitioners lack principled guidance on which algorithm to trust and why they disagree. Prior ensemble methods (Saldanha 2020, Guo et al. 2021, Dai et al. 2004) use global weights, frequency-based voting, or single-family ensembles that ignore the informative structure of per-edge disagreements. The pattern of cross-algorithm disagreement is a rich but underexploited diagnostic signal about which assumptions hold locally in the data.
proposed_approach: ADECD operates in three stages: (1) Portfolio Execution: run 7 diverse algorithms spanning constraint-based, score-based, optimization-based, and functional model families. (2) Per-Edge Assumption Profiling: for each candidate edge, compute statistical tests assessing linearity (HSIC residual test with 500 permutations), Gaussianity (Anderson-Darling test), faithfulness (partial correlations over Markov-blanket-restricted conditioning sets for scalability), and causal sufficiency (PC vs FCI comparison). (3) Diagnostic Reconciliation: compute per-edge confidence as a weighted combination of algorithm outputs, where weights are determined by how well the local assumption profile matches each algorithm's requirements. Weights use 4 transferable beta parameters initialized on synthetic data and adaptively fine-tuned on bootstrap resamples of the target dataset to mitigate synthetic-to-real transfer gaps.

related_work.title: Evaluation of Algorithm Selection and Ensemble Methods for Causal Discovery
related_work.relation: Most directly related: develops a causal ensemble combining multiple algorithm outputs with global weights. ADECD differs by using per-edge assumption profiling rather than global weights, and by providing interpretable diagnostics of why algorithms disagree on each specific edge.

related_work.title: Scalable and Flexible Two-Phase Ensemble Algorithms for Causality Discovery
related_work.relation: Proposes data-partitioning and frequency-based voting for scalable ensemble causal discovery. ADECD differs by using assumption-aware per-edge weighting rather than assumption-agnostic frequency voting.

related_work.title: Ensembling MML Causal Discovery
related_work.relation: Early ensemble work using weighted voting within a single algorithm family (MML). ADECD ensembles across fundamentally different algorithm families with different structural assumptions.

related_work.title: Supervised Ensemble-based Causal DAG Selection
related_work.relation: Uses supervised multilabel classification to select the best DAG from multiple candidates. ADECD instead reconciles at the per-edge level rather than selecting a single DAG, enabling mixed trust across edges.

related_work.title: Assumption violations in causal discovery and the robustness of score matching
related_work.authors: Montagna, Mastakouri, Eulig, Noceti, Rosasco, Janzing, Aragam, Locatello
related_work.relation: Benchmarks algorithms under assumption violations but does not propose a reconciliation method. Our work builds on their finding that different algorithms have different robustness profiles.

related_work.title: A Diagnostic Tool for Functional Causal Discovery
related_work.relation: Introduces CDDR diagnostic for bivariate causal discovery. Our work extends diagnostic analysis to the multivariate multi-algorithm ensemble setting.

related_work.title: Comprehensive Review and Empirical Evaluation of Causal Discovery Algorithms for Numerical Data
related_work.relation: Provides algorithm recommendations based on global dataset metadata. ADECD goes further with per-edge local diagnostics and principled reconciliation.

related_work.title: Bootstrap aggregation and confidence measures to improve time series causal discovery
related_work.relation: Bootstrap approach captures sampling variability of one algorithm. ADECD captures structural variability across algorithm families with different assumptions.

related_work.title: DAGs with NO TEARS: Continuous Optimization for Structure Learning
related_work.relation: Foundational continuous optimization method in our algorithm portfolio.

related_work.title: A Linear Non-Gaussian Acyclic Model for Causal Discovery
related_work.relation: Foundational functional causal model method in our portfolio exploiting non-Gaussianity.

related_work.title: Causation, Prediction, and Search
related_work.relation: Introduced the PC and FCI algorithms, foundational constraint-based methods in our portfolio.

related_work.title: Optimal Structure Identification With Greedy Search
related_work.relation: Introduced GES, a foundational score-based method in our portfolio.

hypothesis: An assumption-diagnostic ensemble that weights algorithm contributions per-edge based on local statistical assessment of assumption satisfaction will (1) produce more accurate causal graphs than any individual algorithm across diverse data-generating conditions, with 5-15% average SHD improvement, (2) outperform naive ensemble methods and global-weight ensembles by 5-10% in F1 score through per-edge assumption profiling, and (3) provide reasonably calibrated confidence scores with Brier score below 0.20.
success_criteria: ADECD achieves statistically significantly lower SHD than the best individual algorithm in at least 50% of tested synthetic settings
ADECD never performs worse than the median individual algorithm in any setting (robustness guarantee)
Per-edge confidence scores are calibrated: observed accuracy within 15% of predicted confidence across all deciles
Assumption profile correctly identifies the dominant assumption violation in >65% of cases
ADECD with per-edge weighting outperforms global-weight ensemble (Saldanha-style) in at least 60% of settings
On real-world benchmarks (Sachs, Asia, Alarm, Child), ADECD matches or exceeds the best individual algorithm without prior knowledge of which to use
Total runtime is no more than 5x the slowest individual algorithm

computational_requirements.justification: All algorithms (PC, FCI, GES, NOTEARS, LiNGAM, CAM) run on CPU for graphs up to 50 nodes. Statistical tests (HSIC with 500 permutations, Anderson-Darling, Markov-blanket-restricted partial correlations) are fast. The grid of ~1000 synthetic experiments (200 settings x 5 seeds) is parallelizable across 2 cores. Bootstrap-adaptive calibration adds ~50 additional algorithm runs but only on the target dataset.
